meaning: adj.(措辞等)恰当的,巧妙的,极为适当的,可喜的,善于措词的,[罕]幸福的,快乐的
examples: Her prose style is not always felicitous; she tends to repetition.
She played him — sometimes delicately, sometimes with a less felicitous touch.
Her choice of music is felicitous.
Granted, the felicitous weather also can hinder home-improvement retailers because of lost sales of winter merchandise.
There is no reason to expect even more misguided unilateral executive branch actions regarding Bosnia to produce more felicitous results.
